whyNotes.nom_sg: Hard masculine inanimates have a zero ending (bare stem) in nominative singular.
whyNotes.gen_sg: Hard masculine inanimates take -u in genitive singular (e.g. hradu).
whyNotes.dat_sg: Hard masculine inanimates take -u in dative singular (e.g. hradu).
whyNotes.acc_sg: Hard masculine inanimates (non-animate) have accusative = nominative, so zero ending.
whyNotes.voc_sg: Hard masculine inanimates take -e in vocative singular, with possible consonant alternation (h>z, k>c, r>ř, ch>š).
whyNotes.loc_sg: Hard masculine inanimates take -ě or -u in locative singular (with possible consonant alternation: h>z, k>c, r>ř, d>ď, t>ť, n>ň).
whyNotes.ins_sg: Hard masculine inanimates take -em in instrumental singular (e.g. hradem).
whyNotes.nom_pl: Hard masculine inanimates take -y in nominative plural (e.g. hrady).
whyNotes.gen_pl: Hard masculine inanimates take -ů in genitive plural (e.g. hradů).
whyNotes.dat_pl: Hard masculine inanimates take -ům in dative plural (e.g. hradům).
whyNotes.acc_pl: Hard masculine inanimates (non-animate) have accusative plural = nominative plural, so -y.
whyNotes.voc_pl: Hard masculine inanimates take -y in vocative plural (same as nominative plural).
whyNotes.loc_pl: Hard masculine inanimates take -ech in locative plural (e.g. hradech), sometimes -ích after soft consonants.
whyNotes.ins_pl: Hard masculine inanimates take -y in instrumental plural (e.g. hrady).
